title: To prohibit the use of Federal funds to implement Salmonella framework for raw poultry products.
stage_label: Referred to the Subcommittee on Livestock, Dairy, and Poultry.
plain_summary: This bill stops the federal government from using taxpayer money to put in place new rules about Salmonella bacteria in raw chicken and turkey. The Department of Agriculture proposed these rules in August 2024 to make chicken and turkey safer by testing for harmful bacteria and keeping better records. The new rules would say that chicken and turkey with too much Salmonella bacteria cannot be sold in stores. This bill would prevent those safety rules from happening by not allowing the government to spend money on them.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Livestock, Dairy, and Poultry.

sections.title: Section 1 Prohibition on Use of Federal Funds to Implement Salmonella Framework for Raw Poultry Products
sections.text: The federal government cannot spend any money to finish, put into action, manage, or enforce a proposed rule about Salmonella bacteria in raw chicken and other poultry products. This rule was called the Salmonella Framework for Raw Poultry Products. It was published by the Food Safety and Inspection Service, which is part of the Department of Agriculture. The rule was published in the Federal Register on August seventh, two thousand twenty four.
